mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation expectations, as indicated by a 1-month increase in CPIAUCSL above 0.5%, signal central bank tightening pressures that depress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust supply-side expansion, which drives corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 for any 3-year window, or 20-day returns in co-moving INDPRO/HOUST periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: High unemployment (UNRATE > 6.3%) combined with rising payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates labor market recovery, which boosts consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where UNRATE > 6.3% and PAYEMS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects strong aggregate demand, which drives corporate profits and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in GDPC1 growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real sales (RSAFS) above 500,000 million dollars signals strong consumer demand, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where RSAFS exceeds 500,000 million dollars.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicate monetary tightening amid inflation, which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 for any 3-year window, or 20-day returns in co-moving FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals supply-side contraction, which reduces corporate earnings and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines by more than 1% in 2 months.
